title: refund — published events
description: Every event implements `ShouldDispatchAfterCommit` so listeners never fire against a rolled-back write. Two events are CRITICAL: `RefundSucceeded` (P1 signal — the finalization chain fires credit-note + transaction + clawback fan-out + marketing negative-conversion), and `RefundFailed` / `RefundProviderError` (P1 signal — merchant fails to reverse a legitimate refund request, exposing customer-service + regulator risk). Financial events (`RefundApproved` / `RefundSucceeded` / `RefundClawbackReferral` / `RefundClawbackCoupon`) additionally fan out to finance::invoice for state update + finance::transaction for ledger offset + finance::credit-note (via invoice module) for reversal document. `RefundSucceeded` fires a marketing negative-value conversion downstream to protect ROAS + retrain fraud models on ad networks.

events.name: RefundRequested
events.trigger: RefundRepository finished a successful create. Fires exactly once per refund. Distinct from `RefundApproved` — this fires unconditionally at initiation, before approval routing.

events.name: RefundApprovalRequired
events.trigger: RefundObserver.creating computed `requires_approval=true` (amount / staleness threshold OR always-approve reason). Distinct from RefundRequested (which fires unconditionally). Dispatches NotifyRefundApprovalRequiredJob to seed approver notifications.

events.name: RefundApproved
events.trigger: Refund.status transitioned awaiting_approval -> approved. Approver clicked approve OR requires_approval=false path auto-transitioned at creation.

events.name: RefundRejected
events.trigger: Refund.status transitioned awaiting_approval -> rejected. Approver clicked reject. Terminal state — no provider dispatch.

events.name: RefundProcessing
events.trigger: Refund.status transitioned approved -> processing. InitiateProviderRefundJob dispatched the provider-side createRefund call + received an async 'processing' response.
consumers: notifications::DispatchRefundProcessedNotification (customer — money on the way, ~5-10 days for card refunds)

events.name: RefundSucceeded
events.trigger: Refund.status transitioned processing -> succeeded. Provider webhook OR CheckRefundProviderStatusJob confirmed terminal success. FIRES THE FINALIZATION CHAIN: credit note creation + offsetting transaction + invoice state update + clawback fan-out + marketing negative-conversion. P1 event.
consumers: notifications::DispatchRefundSucceededNotification (customer — money received; note: for cards, this is estimated delivery not exact)

events.name: RefundFailed
events.trigger: Refund.status transitioned processing -> failed. Provider returned a non-recoverable error (original charge already refunded, provider account closed, source no longer valid). Terminal. Customer + admin notified. Payment untouched.

events.name: RefundCancelled
events.trigger: Refund.status transitioned awaiting_approval | approved -> cancelled. Cancel path — customer cancelled their own request OR admin cancelled before provider dispatch. Terminal.

events.name: RefundLineCreated
events.trigger: RefundLineRepository finished a successful create. Fires per line at partial / prorated refund creation. Distinct from RefundRequested (parent) — one parent request emits one RefundRequested + N RefundLineCreated events.

events.name: RefundClawbackReferral
events.trigger: RefundSucceeded fired + ClawbackCoordinator detected that the refunded invoice was linked to a materialized ReferralReward. Cascades to growth::referrals for reward reversal.
consumers: referrals::ProcessReferralClawback (Wave 5 — reverses the reward + notifies the referrer; coupon-rewards cascade further to coupon::ProcessCouponClawback)

events.name: RefundClawbackCoupon
events.trigger: RefundSucceeded fired + ClawbackCoordinator detected that the refunded invoice had a redeemed coupon. Cascades to coupon module for redemption reversal.

events.name: RefundNotifiedProvider
events.trigger: InitiateProviderRefundJob successfully called the provider's createRefund method and received a 2xx response. Distinct from RefundProcessing (which fires on the async 'processing' state after the call) — this fires immediately after the HTTP call succeeds.

events.name: RefundProviderError
events.trigger: Refund.status transitioned processing -> provider_error. Provider returned a non-terminal error (network timeout, transient outage, rate limit) — the refund is retryable via ReconcileRefundsJob or manual force-fail after investigation. P1 signal — actionable to ops.
consumers: notifications::DispatchRefundStatusNotification (admin only — do not alarm customer until terminal)

events.name: RefundProviderReconciled
events.trigger: Provider webhook (via payment::PaymentWebhookHandler dispatching a refund-status webhook) OR ReconcileRefundsJob detected a state change on the provider side. The refund's status is now aligned with the provider's authoritative record. Distinct from RefundSucceeded / RefundFailed — this event fires FIRST (before terminal transition) to record the reconciliation itself.

payload.drift_detected: boolean (true when the local status differed from provider)
